title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
IT Department helps Pay Taxes Online and Track Income Tax Refunds
Paying taxes might not be the most exciting task on your to-do list, but the Income Tax Department of India has been working hard to make it as seamless as possible.  From levying taxes to ensuring smooth collection and assessment, the department has introduced multiple user-friendly features over the years. Read on as we discuss how the IT department simplifies paying taxes and tracking income tax refunds through its online portals. 1
IT department: An overview
The Income Tax Department, which is an integral part of the Indian government looks into the assessment and collection of income taxes in the country. It is responsible for the following tasks:1
Tax collections
Primarily, the income tax department has the responsibility of gathering income tax from individuals, local authorities or businesses who are liable to pay taxes as per the Income Tax Act, of 1961.
Enforcement of tax laws
In addition to imposing taxes, the department is also responsible for looking into the enforcement of various tax laws in the country like the Income Tax Act of 1961, the Black Money Act of 2015 and the Benami Transactions Act of 1988.
Processing returns
The IT department takes the responsibility of ensuring a seamless filing of income tax returns. Know that the ITRs are processed at the Centralised Processing Center (CPC).
Managing international taxationThe IT department ensures to look into international tax matters to avoid double taxation.
Spreading awarenessThe IT department also ensures to offer support to taxpayers by providing information about the tax liabilities, rights and responsibilities under various tax laws in the country. Additionally, the department also undertakes to spread general awareness of income tax laws.
How do you pay taxes online within minutes?
The IT department has made it easier to pay income taxes online through its online portal. Here’s how you can pay income taxes online:2
Step 1: Visit the online portal of the income tax department and find the “e-Pay Tax” option to open a new tab for filing taxes.
Step 2: Fill in the necessary details like PAN and mobile number. Verify your profile through a simple OPT verification.
Step 3: In this step, you have to select the assessment year and the preferred payment type. Make sure to select the “Income Tax” box to choose the assessment year and then proceed to select “Self-Assessment Tax (300)” under the “Type of Payment”.
Step 4: Now, you have to enter the payment details and then select the payment method. Remember that the IT department allows payment through debit card, credit card, internet banking, UPI, RTGS/NEFT or pay at the bank counter.
Step 5: Once you have made the paymen,t you will receive a confirmation.
How to claim Income tax refunds: Easy online tracking?
Before we move ahead with the process of tracking income tax refunds, first, know that an income tax refund is granted to you by the tax department in case you have paid more income tax than you were supposed to pay in the given financial year.3
Once you have filed your income tax returns, the IT department looks into your tax liabilities. If the amount paid exceeds the liability,  the department issues a return along with interest at 0.5% per month or 6% per annum till the time you have received the refund. 3
Here’s how you can track your income tax refunds through the income tax portal:3
Step 1: Visit the online income tax portal and log into your account.
Step 2: Select the “e-file” menu and from the dropbox, click on the “Income Tax Returns” and then select the “View Filed Returns” option.
Step 3: You will be able to see the status of the ITR you have filed. Select the “View Details” option to check the status of your income tax refunds.
Here’s how you can track your income tax refunds through the NSDL portal:3
Step 1: Visit the official NSDL portal and log into your account using your PAN details.
Step 2: Once you are in, select the assessment year for which you want to check the status of income tax refunds. Verify the login using a capture code.
Step 3: Select the “Proceed” option under the “Taxpayer Refund” bar, and you will be able to see your refund status.
Conclusion
The tax system in India finds its roots in ancient writings like the Manusmriti, and over the years, the income tax department has adopted unique features that have made that assessment and collection simpler. The income tax department is crucial in ensuring advance to tax laws in the country.1
FAQs
What is the IT department?
The IT department in India looks into the assessment and collection of income taxes in the country and it is an integral part of the central government. The IT department, in addition to assessing and collecting income taxes, also looks into the enforcement of tax various tax laws and international taxations. 1
Can I file income tax returns online?
Yes, you can file your income tax returns online through the official Income Tax Portal.2
How can I check the status of my income tax refunds online?
You can check the status of your income tax refunds either by visiting the official income tax portal or NSDL portal by simply logging in and selecting the assessment year for which you want to check the refund status. 3
Sources
https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/definition/income-tax-department?from=mdr
https://cleartax.in/s/pay-income-tax-due
https://cleartax.in/s/income-tax-refund

The views expressed in this article is not to be construed as professional advice and users are advised to seek independent professional/expert advice before making any decisions based on the same. full_pdf_text:
IT Department helps Pay Taxes Online and Track Income Tax Refunds
Paying taxes might not be the most exciting task on your to-do list, but the Income Tax Department of
India has been working hard to make it as seamless as possible. From levying taxes to ensuring smooth
collection and assessment, the department has introduced multiple user-friendly features over the
years. Read on as we discuss how the IT department simplifies paying taxes and tracking income tax
refunds through its online portals. 1
IT department: An overview
The Income Tax Department, which is an integral part of the Indian government looks into the
assessment and collection of income taxes in the country. It is responsible for the following tasks:1
1. Tax collections
Primarily, the income tax department has the responsibility of gathering income tax from
individuals, local authorities or businesses who are liable to pay taxes as per the Income Tax Act,
of 1961.
2. Enforcement of tax laws
In addition to imposing taxes, the department is also responsible for looking into the
enforcement of various tax laws in the country like the Income Tax Act of 1961, the Black Money
Act of 2015 and the Benami Transactions Act of 1988.
3. Processing returns
The IT department takes the responsibility of ensuring a seamless filing of income tax returns.
Know that the ITRs are processed at the Centralised Processing Center (CPC).
4. Managing international taxation
The IT department ensures to look into international tax matters to avoid double taxation.
5. Spreading awareness
The IT department also ensures to offer support to taxpayers by providing information about
the tax liabilities, rights and responsibilities under various tax laws in the country. Additionally,
the department also undertakes to spread general awareness of income tax laws.
How do you pay taxes online within minutes?
The IT department has made it easier to pay income taxes online through its online portal. Here’s how
you can pay income taxes online:2
● Step 1: Visit the online portal of the income tax department and find the “e-Pay Tax” option to
open a new tab for filing taxes.
● Step 2: Fill in the necessary details like PAN and mobile number. Verify your profile through a
simple OPT verification.
● Step 3: In this step, you have to select the assessment year and the preferred payment type.
Make sure to select the “Income Tax” box to choose the assessment year and then proceed to
select “Self-Assessment Tax (300)” under the “Type of Payment”.
● Step 4: Now, you have to enter the payment details and then select the payment method.
Remember that the IT department allows payment through debit card, credit card, internet
banking, UPI, RTGS/NEFT or pay at the bank counter.
● Step 5: Once you have made the paymen,t you will receive a confirmation.
How to claim Income tax refunds: Easy online tracking?
Before we move ahead with the process of tracking income tax refunds, first, know that an income tax
refund is granted to you by the tax department in case you have paid more income tax than you were
supposed to pay in the given financial year.3
Once you have filed your income tax returns, the IT department looks into your tax liabilities. If the
amount paid exceeds the liability, the department issues a return along with interest at 0.5% per month
or 6% per annum till the time you have received the refund. 3
Here’s how you can track your income tax refunds through the income tax portal:3
● Step 1: Visit the online income tax portal and log into your account.
● Step 2: Select the “e-file” menu and from the dropbox, click on the “Income Tax Returns” and
then select the “View Filed Returns” option.
● Step 3: You will be able to see the status of the ITR you have filed. Select the “View Details”
option to check the status of your income tax refunds.
Here’s how you can track your income tax refunds through the NSDL portal:3
● Step 1: Visit the official NSDL portal and log into your account using your PAN details.
● Step 2: Once you are in, select the assessment year for which you want to check the status of
income tax refunds. Verify the login using a capture code.
● Step 3: Select the “Proceed” option under the “Taxpayer Refund” bar, and you will be able to
see your refund status.
Conclusion
The tax system in India finds its roots in ancient writings like the Manusmriti, and over the years, the
income tax department has adopted unique features that have made that assessment and collection
simpler. The income tax department is crucial in ensuring advance to tax laws in the country.1
FAQs
1. What is the IT department?
The IT department in India looks into the assessment and collection of income taxes in the
country and it is an integral part of the central government. The IT department, in addition to
assessing and collecting income taxes, also looks into the enforcement of tax various tax laws
and international taxations. 1
2. Can I file income tax returns online?
Yes, you can file your income tax returns online through the official Income Tax Portal.2
3. How can I check the status of my income tax refunds online?
You can check the status of your income tax refunds either by visiting the official income tax
portal or NSDL portal by simply logging in and selecting the assessment year for which you want
to check the refund status. 3
Sources
1. https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/definition/income-tax-department?from=mdr
2. https://cleartax.in/s/pay-income-tax-due
3. https://cleartax.in/s/income-tax-refund
